primary_concern: Sepsis with concurrent acute kidney injury (AKI) and coagulopathy
clinical_summary: Elevated lactate (4.2 mmol/L) combined with leukocytosis (14.2 K/uL) and low hemoglobin (9.8 g/dL) meet Sepsis-3 criteria for sepsis risk. Platelets are persistently below 100 K/uL (88-95 K/uL), fulfilling SOFA coagulation scoring thresholds. Bilirubin elevation (1.4 mg/dL) and progressive AKI staging (Stage 1 to Stage 3) suggest multi-organ dysfunction. The excluded creatinine outlier (8.9 mg/dL) indicates severe AKI, though Stage 3 criteria require clinical validation.

prioritized_risks.explanation: Lactate >2 mmol/L, WBC >12 K/uL, and elevated creatinine meet Sepsis-3 criteria (suspected infection + SOFA increase ≥2)
Creatinine elevation consistent with Stage 3 AKI (excluded outlier value 8.9 mg/dL requires clinical correlation)

prioritized_risks.guideline_source: Laboratory Reference Ranges and Clinical Thresholds
prioritized_risks.explanation: Platelets <100 K/uL meets SOFA coagulopathy scoring (platelets <150 K/uL = SOFA score 1)
Lactate >2 mmol/L present but hypotension and vasopressor requirements unknown
prioritized_risks.threshold: Vasopressor-dependent hypotension + lactate >2 mmol/L after fluids

recommended_actions: Initiate broad-spectrum antibiotics per sepsis protocol
Perform fluid resuscitation and reassess perfusion status
Order blood cultures and source control evaluation
Monitor renal function with hourly creatinine and urine output
Consider platelet transfusion if <50 K/uL or active bleeding
doctor_handoff: Review for source of infection (focus on abdominal/urinary sources given bilirubin elevation). Confirm vasopressor requirements for septic shock exclusion. Reassess AKI stage with corrected creatinine values.

disease_progression.observation: Initial sepsis markers: WBC 14.2 K/uL, Hgb 9.8 g/dL
AKI Stage 1 (creatinine 2.1 mg/dL) and platelets 95 K/uL
Lactate rises to 4.2 mmol/L with platelets 88 K/uL
Platelets remain low at 92 K/uL; AKI Stage 3 (excluded creatinine 8.9 mg/dL)

excluded_outliers.reason: Rapid change detected — Creatinine changed by 6.5 mg/dL within 6.0h (max allowed: 2.0). Probable measurement error or specimen issue. Section 15.2 — rise > 2.0 mg/dL in < 6h.
